name: Camino Primitivo, Ruta de Los Hospitales, Alto de La Marta
region_intro: Hiking around Tineo offers diverse terrain in the heart of western Asturias, characterized by its mountainous landscape and varied natural features. The region includes fertile valleys, native forests of beech and oak, and river paths, notably along the Nonaya and Narcea Rivers. Its elevation changes provide a range of hiking experiences, from gentle walks to more challenging ascents and descents. Best hiking trails around Tineo The most popular hiking route is Camino Primitivo: Tineo and Letizia Viewpoint Loop, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views and follows a section of the historic Camino Primitivo. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino Primitivo, Ruta de Los Hospitales, Alto de La Marta, a difficult 12.8 miles (20.6 km) path. This trail features significant elevation changes and traverses mountainous terrain with expansive views. Local hikers also love the Tineo to El Pedregal — circular route, a 13.9 miles (22.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and ancient forests, often completed in about 6 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Tineo is defined by mountainous terrain, ancient forests, and river valleys, with traditional villages often encountered along the way. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Tineo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Tineo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Tineo?
A: Tineo offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 45 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to challenging long-distance treks, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Tineo?
A: Hiking in Tineo is characterized by its mountainous landscape in western Asturias. You'll encounter fertile valleys, ancient forests of beech and oak, and paths along rivers like the Nonaya and Esva. The region features significant elevation changes, offering both gentle slopes and more demanding ascents and descents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Tineo?
A: Yes, Tineo has several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. There are 10 easy routes available on komoot, often leading through picturesque villages or along river paths. These trails typically have less elevation gain and are shorter in duration.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Tineo?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Tineo are designed as circular paths, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048591733/tineo-and-the-letizia-viewpoint-on-the-primitive-way-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tineo and the Letizia Viewpoint on the Primitive Way — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views and is part of the historic Camino Primitivo.

Q: Are there challenging long-distance hikes in Tineo?
A: For experienced hikers, Tineo offers challenging long-distance routes, particularly sections of the historic Camino Primitivo. These trails feature significant elevation changes and traverse mountainous terrain. An example is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048586833/camino-primitivo-ruta-de-los-hospitales-alto-de-la-marta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Primitivo, Ruta de Los Hospitales, Alto de La Marta&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 20 km with substantial climbs.

Q: What unique natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Tineo?
A: Tineo's trails lead to several notable natural features and landmarks. You might encounter the ancient and grand &lt;strong&gt;Carbayo de Valentín&lt;/strong&gt;, a designated Natural Monument, or the unique geological formation of &lt;strong&gt;Fana de Genestaza&lt;/strong&gt;. The gorges formed by the &lt;strong&gt;Hoces del Río Esva&lt;/strong&gt; are also recognized for their ecological value. Additionally, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4506626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Roque of Tineo&lt;/a&gt;, a 14th-century chapel on the Jacobean route.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with spectacular scenery in Tineo?
A: Yes, Tineo is home to several viewpoints offering breathtaking panoramic views. The Letizia Viewpoint is a popular spot, often included in circular hiking routes from Tineo. Another excellent location is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2873224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Marta&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive vistas of the surrounding mountains and valleys.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Tineo?
A: The hiking trails in Tineo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 115 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, from ancient forests to river valleys, and the opportunity to experience sections of the historic Camino Primitivo. The well-preserved rural atmosphere and traditional villages encountered along the way are also often highlighted.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Tineo?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always available, many of the 10 easy routes in Tineo are suitable for families. These trails typically involve less strenuous terrain and shorter distances, making them enjoyable for children. Look for routes that pass through villages or along gentle river paths for a pleasant family outing.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Tineo?
A: The best season for hiking in Tineo is generally spring and autumn, when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn offers stunning fall foliage in the region's extensive beech and oak forests. Summer can also be pleasant, especially at higher elevations, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear due to potential snow and colder conditions in mountainous areas.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Tineo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most public hiking trails in Tineo, especially in natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste. Some protected areas or specific private properties might have restrictions, so it's good practice to check for signage.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through traditional Asturian villages?
A: Yes, many hiking routes in Tineo will lead you through small, traditional Asturian villages. These offer a glimpse into the local rural atmosphere, where you can often spot traditional elements like 'hórreos' (granaries). The Camino Primitivo, in particular, passes through several such settlements, providing cultural insights alongside natural beauty.
tips: If you are doing the Camino Primitivo, this is the first of the three hospitals that you will see on this alternative itinerary. Although very little remains of this ancient building, part of the structure can still be seen.

Highest point of the route of the hospitals and of the entire Camino Primitivo. If you have brought something from home you can leave it here, as the pilgrims of the French Way do at the Cruz de Ferro.
Although only a mound of stones remains, this hospital served pilgrims on the Camino de Santiago until the 19th century. The setting is spectacular.
Port with an altitude of 1105 meters and with very good views – it can be seen from the Cantabrian Mountains to the Cantabrian Sea.
It is an old water deposit that was used to extract gold. Cows and horses are often seen grazing on the banks, which gives this space an additional charm. From here there is a spectacular 360º view.

Ruins of the Hospital of Valparaíso
